description_long: This is a small crab with a robust, rounded carapace and two prominent claws, one of which is significantly larger than the other, typical of fiddler crabs. The carapace is dark brown with subtle mottling, while the claws are lighter, almost creamy white with brownish speckles. The crab has eight visible walking legs, slender and jointed, extending from the sides of the body. Its eyes are positioned on short stalks at the front corners of the carapace, giving it a wide field of view. The surface of the crab appears hard and slightly glossy, with a rough, textured finish on the carapace and smoother claws. The overall size is small, as indicated by the color calibration cube for scale.
description: A small, dark brown crab with one oversized pale claw, eight slender legs, and eyes on short stalks. The carapace is hard and textured, with a glossy finish on the claws.
description_view_0: Front view showing both claws raised, with the larger claw on the left and the carapace and legs spread wide.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the oversized claw and the slender walking legs, with the eyes visible on short stalks.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the rounded carapace and the symmetrical arrangement of the legs.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the smaller claw and the curvature of the body and legs.
